ops.text:
RESULTS (n_pairs=470, DPO train view). Spend $0 (pure compute, no LLM). Instrument spot-checked: smallest-d pairs are genuine near-dupes (id=1cc23e3f d=0.008 — chosen/rejected differ only in the ```old``` diff block being populated vs empty, the exact DPOP small-edit pattern), d==0 pairs are truly degenerate (chosen==rejected verbatim, e.g. id=49cc83ec), median-d pair (0.77) is genuinely different content. Metric correct.

PRIMARY (word tok, `\w+|[^\w\s]`) normalized edit distance:
  quantiles min/p5/p10/p25/p50/p75/p90/p95/max = 0.00/0.24/0.36/0.58/0.77/0.88/0.94/0.96/1.00
  mean 0.700. Distribution heavily right-skewed toward LARGE distances (mode bin 0.85-0.90).
  identical (d==0) share 1.49% (7 pairs); near-identical (d<0.05) 2.77%.
  small_edit_share (d<0.20) = 0.0489 (23/470), bootstrap 95% CI [0.0298, 0.0681].

ROBUSTNESS — small_edit_share across 4 tokenizers, ALL far below 30%:
  word 4.89% | whitespace 3.19% | char 4.89% | Qwen3-8B subword 4.47%. Range 3.2-4.9%.

PRE-REGISTERED DECISION: small_edit_share 4.9% << 30% threshold => bakeoff_warranted = FALSE.
RECOMMENDATION: SKIP the DPOP arm. The DPO-Positive small-edit failure mode is present but rare (<5% of pairs); it does not justify the extra DPOP bake-off arm. Standard DPO/KTO are not impacted by this pathology (their small-edit exposure is the same <5%). Decision robust to tokenizer choice and bootstrap CI (upper bound 6.8% << 30%).

SIDE FINDING (data quality, unprompted): 7 pairs (1.5%) have chosen==rejected token-identically (5 char-exact) — degenerate zero-signal preference pairs that should be filtered from ANY preference view (DPO/KTO alike), independent of the DPOP question. Small-edit pairs concentrate in category=direction (7/121) and unwanted_action (6/31); the ```old``` diff-block-population artifact is the dominant near-dup mechanism.

Artifacts (out/): summary.json, per_pair.jsonl (470 rows, per-tokenizer d), histogram.txt, analyze.py. Verify lane: re-run analyze.py under `uv run --no-sync --with rapidfuzz`.
